info.title: subsln
info.description:
Command line tool used to manipulate .subsln files and released as .NET SDK tool.

Check our project page to see how install it.
If you need the .NET libraries, use the others SubSolution NuGet packages.

commands.name: create
commands.description: Create a new .subsln SubSolution configuration file

options.name: --force
options.description: Force to overwrite existing files, without asking user.

options.name: --open
options.description: Open .sln file with its default application.

options.name: --xsd
options.description:
Set the XSD schema location. Allow to have auto-completion
in your XML editor even if it doesn't recognize the XML
namespace.

options.name: --debug
options.description: Enable debug mode to show exceptions and callstacks.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

arguments.name: files
arguments.description:
Paths of the files to create, relative to working
directory. If no extension provided, default extension is
".subsln".

commands.name: generate
commands.description: Generate .sln Visual Studio solution files from .subsln files.

options.name: --preview
options.description:
Preview the command result without creating/updating
files.

options.name: --force
options.description: Force to apply changes, without asking user.

options.name: --new
options.description:
Create new solutions from scratch instead of updating
existing ones.

options.name: --open
options.description: Open generated files with their default application.

options.name: --show
options.description: Show representation of generated solutions.

options.name: --detailed
options.description: Show more details in solution output.

options.name: --divergent
options.description:
Show divergent projects contexts in solution output
(divergent configuration-platforms, disabled build,
disabled deploy).

options.name: --paths
options.description: Show project paths in solution output.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose log (solution build, project reading, ...)

options.name: --dotnet
options.description: Read projects using given dotnet directory path.

options.name: --msbuild
options.description: Read projects using given MSBuild directory path.

options.name: --embedded
options.description: Read projects using embedded MSBuild DLLs.

options.name: --import-fallback
options.description:
On missing imports or SDks, read manually
Directory.Build.props and use fallback values. Result may
differ from reality.

options.name: --debug
options.description: Enable debug mode to show exceptions and callstacks.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

arguments.name: files
arguments.description:
Paths of SubSolution configuration files to generate.
Also accept relative file path using glob patterns ("*",
"**" and "?"). If not provided, all .subsln files in
working directory and its sub-directories will be
generated.

commands.name: show
commands.description: Show the content of .sln or .subsln files.

options.name: --detailed
options.description: Show more details in solution output.

options.name: --divergent
options.description:
Show divergent projects contexts in solution output
(divergent configuration-platforms, disabled build,
disabled deploy).

options.name: --paths
options.description: Show project paths in solution output.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose log (solution build, project reading, ...)

options.name: --dotnet
options.description: Read projects using given dotnet directory path.

options.name: --msbuild
options.description: Read projects using given MSBuild directory path.

options.name: --embedded
options.description: Read projects using embedded MSBuild DLLs.

options.name: --import-fallback
options.description:
On missing imports or SDks, read manually
Directory.Build.props and use fallback values. Result may
differ from reality.

options.name: --debug
options.description: Enable debug mode to show exceptions and callstacks.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

arguments.name: files
arguments.description:
Paths of the solution files to show. Can be
.sln or .subsln files.

commands.name: validate
commands.description: Validate .subsln files and their associated .sln files are up-to-date.

options.name: --show
options.description: Show representation of validated solution.

options.name: --detailed
options.description: Show more details in solution output.

options.name: --divergent
options.description:
Show divergent projects contexts in solution output
(divergent configuration-platforms, disabled build,
disabled deploy).

options.name: --paths
options.description: Show project paths in solution output.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Enable verbose log (solution build, project reading, ...)

options.name: --dotnet
options.description: Read projects using given dotnet directory path.

options.name: --msbuild
options.description: Read projects using given MSBuild directory path.

options.name: --embedded
options.description: Read projects using embedded MSBuild DLLs.

options.name: --import-fallback
options.description:
On missing imports or SDks, read manually
Directory.Build.props and use fallback values. Result may
differ from reality.

options.name: --debug
options.description: Enable debug mode to show exceptions and callstacks.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

arguments.name: files
arguments.description:
Paths of SubSolution configuration files to validate.
Also accept relative file path pattern using wildcards
"*", "**" and "?". If not provided, all .subsln files in
working directory and its sub-directories will be
validated.
